notes.anchor: M. CICERO Q. PHILIPPO PROCOS. S.
notes.note: The letter-heading; addressed to Q. Marcius Philippus, proconsul (PROCOS. = proconsule) of an eastern province in the mid-40s BC. The first of two letters to him (Fam. 13.73-74); he is distinct from L. Marcius Philippus, cos. 91.

notes.anchor: With Antipater of Derbe there subsists, on my side, not only the tie of guest-friendship but the closest familiarity
notes.note: Antipater was the dynast of Derbe in Lycaonia, in the interior of southern Asia Minor. Hospitium (guest-friendship) was a formal, hereditary bond of mutual hospitality and obligation between Romans and foreign notables, here reinforced by personal intimacy.

notes.anchor: I ask you again and again to grant me his sons, who are in your power
notes.note: Antipater's sons were being held as hostages or prisoners after the proconsul fell out with their father. Cicero asks that they be "granted" (condones) to him as a personal favour, while carefully leaving the governor an honourable way to refuse if his standing would suffer.
